title: Data Analysis and Decision Support
text:
NoteAbout me




PhD candidate in humanities and social sciences transitioning into data analysis

#data, #analysis, #insight

title: Data Analysis and Decision Support
text:
Who I Am
Joint PhD candidate (EPHE-PSL / UCLouvain), I develop expertise in the structured analysis of complex data.
My profile stands out through a methodological approach: I do not focus on tools alone, but on building rigorous, interpretable, and decision-oriented analyses.
I am transitioning into data analysis with a central motivation:
to extract meaning from complex data by identifying relationships, patterns, and underlying structures.
I place particular emphasis on translating these results — through structuring, visualization, and storytelling — to make them immediately understandable and actionable.

title: Data Analysis and Decision Support
text:
What I Can Bring

Structuring and clarifying complex problems

Data cleaning, transformation, and reliability

Rigorous analysis grounded in statistical methods

Data visualization and storytelling

Translating analyses into insights understandable by non-technical stakeholders

Bridging the gap between data and teams to support decision-making

title: CV
text:
Université Paris Sciences et Lettres (PSL)
PhD Candidate – Data analysis and complex data structuring
Paris | 2023–2026

Built and structured multi-source datasets (cleaning, normalization, disambiguation).
Designed relational and graph data models (SQLite, Neo4j).
Developed analytical workflows in R (tidyverse): transformation, aggregation, EDA.
Produced reproducible analytical reports (Quarto).
Conducted network analysis to identify structures and patterns.

title: CV
text:
Université Paris Nanterre
Teaching Assistant – Data analysis and modeling
Nanterre | 2025

Taught data structuring and variable formalization.
Introduced modeling of relationships between variables.
Trained students in applied statistical methods (t-tests, ANOVA, OLS regression, PCA).
Supervised analysis, interpretation, and reporting.

title: CV
text:
Université Paris Lumières
Operations Manager – Process analysis and optimization
Campus Condorcet | 2023

Analyzed and optimized organizational processes.
Defined and monitored key performance indicators (KPIs).
Structured and improved reliability of data flows.
Coordinated stakeholders and managed operations.

title: Approach
text:
My approach combines analytical rigor, data structuring, and actionable insight generation.
I primarily work with complex, heterogeneous, or poorly structured data, using exploration, modeling, and interpretation methods.
The objective is consistent: transforming data into clear and decision-oriented insights.




Exploratory data analysis (EDA) using R / Python

Identification of structures, anomalies, and biases

Text data analysis (qualitative and quantitative)

Integration of heterogeneous data sources

This phase focuses on understanding the internal logic of the data before modeling.





Data cleaning, normalization, and disambiguation

Structuring complex datasets (tabular and relational)

Database modeling (SQL, Neo4j / Cypher)

Building usable datasets

I place strong emphasis on handling messy or incomplete data requiring advanced structuring.





Descriptive and inferential statistics

Modeling relationships between variables (regression, PCA, etc.)

Network and relational analysis

Critical interpretation of results

My approach is grounded in solid statistical and econometric foundations (including Wooldridge).





Data visualization (R, ggplot2, BI tools in progress)

Identification of patterns and relationships

Clear and structured representations

Visualization is central to both analysis and communication.





Translating analysis into actionable insights

Structuring results into clear narratives

Adapting communication to non-technical stakeholders

Concise and effective presentation

I view analysis as a bridge between data and teams, supporting informed decision-making.





R (tidyverse, ggplot2)

Python (pandas, numpy)

SQL (in progress)

Neo4j / Cypher

Excel (advanced)

Power BI (in progress), Tableau (in progress)

RStudio / Posit, Jupyter, VS Code






Handling complex and poorly structured data

Text data analysis (qualitative & quantitative)

Advanced data structuring

Strong methodological rigor

Ability to communicate insights clearly

Strong autonomy in analytical work and learning

Rapid ability to adopt new tools, languages, and methods

My positioning combines:

rigorous analysis → structuring → insight → communication.
